title: SP3 Protocol for Protein Extraction from Archaeological Cooking Vessels
author: Virginia L Harvey

steps.title: Preheat a heat block to 65°C
steps.instruction: Preheat a heat block to 65°C.

steps.title: Add 150 µL 6 M GuHCl to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL 6 M GuHCl to each sample.

steps.title: Briefly vortex to homogenise and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex to homogenise and centrifuge for 1 minute at 13k RPM.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 65°C for 1...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 65°C for 1 hour on the heat block to extract the proteins from the foodcrust into the GuHCl. Then remove the samples from the heat block.

steps.title: Preheat a heat block to 99°C
steps.instruction: Preheat a heat block to 99°C.

steps.title: Add 15 µL of a solution...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL of a solution containing 100 mM TCEP and 100 mM CAA.

steps.title: Briefly vortex to homogenise and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex to homogenise and centrifuge for 1 minute at 13k RPM.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 99°C for 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 99°C for 10 minutes in a heat block, then remove the samples from the heat block and allow to cool down at room temperature for roughly 5 minutes.

steps.title: Preheat a Thermomixer or equivalent heating...
steps.instruction: Preheat a Thermomixer or equivalent heating and shaking device to 24°C or room temperature.

steps.title: Add 500 µg of beads (i
steps.instruction: Add 500 µg of beads (i.e. 10 µL of a 50 µg/µL bead solution in ultrapure water) to each sample. Homogenise through gentle aspirating and dispensing with a pipette in the sample. Note: the ratio of protein to bead concentration should be 1:10 for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add 175 µL 100% ethanol to...
steps.instruction: Add 175 µL 100% ethanol to each sample and homogenise through pipetting. Note: added ethanol volume should be equal to volume currently in the sample tube.

steps.title: Incubate samples in a Thermomixer at...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples in a Thermomixer at 24°C at 1000 RPM for 5 minutes. The proteins will now bind to the magnetic beads.

steps.title: Place the samples on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the samples on the magnetic separation rack and allow the beads to fully migrate for at least 2 minutes.

steps.title: With the samples still on the...
steps.instruction: With the samples still on the magnetic rack, remove the supernatant. Note: the supernatant can be discarded or saved to test for bead extraction efficiency.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the magnetic rack and add 500 µL of 80% ethanol. Resuspend the beads through gentle pipetting. Then place the beads back on the magnetic rack and allow the beads to migrate for at least 2 minutes.

steps.title: With the beads on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: With the beads on the magnetic rack, remove the supernatant and discard to waste.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the magnetic rack and add 300 µL of 80% ethanol. Resuspend the beads through gentle pipetting. Then place the beads back on the magnetic rack and allow the beads to migrate for at least 2 minutes.

steps.title: With the beads on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: With the beads on the magnetic rack, remove the supernatant and discard to waste.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the magnetic rack and add 200 µL of 80% ethanol. Resuspend the beads through gentle pipetting. Then place the beads back on the magnetic rack and allow the beads to migrate for at least 2 minutes.

steps.title: With the beads on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: With the beads on the magnetic rack, remove the supernatant and discard to waste. Ensure that as much supernatant as possible is removed.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads in 100 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads in 100 µL 50 mM ABC to elute the proteins from the beads and homogenise through gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Place the samples on a thermomixer...
steps.instruction: Place the samples on a thermomixer at 37°C and 750 RPM for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Place the samples on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the samples on the magnetic rack and allow the beads to migrate for at least 2 minutes.

steps.title: Remove 10 µL and store separately for protein quantification (i
steps.instruction: Remove 10 µL and store separately for protein quantification (i.e. Bradford assay or BCA).

steps.title: Resuspend 20 µg of Sequencing grade...
steps.instruction: Resuspend 20 µg of Sequencing grade Trypsin Promega Catalog #V5111 with 100 µL of resuspension buffer to create a 0.2 µg/µL solution. Add 1 µL of trypsin solution to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate the samples at 37°C and...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples at 37°C and 750 RPM for 18 hours to digest.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the mixer...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the mixer and centrifuge at 13k RPM for 1 minute.

steps.title: Place the samples on the magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place the samples on the magnetic rack and allow the beads to migrate for at least 2 minutes. Transfer all the supernatant to new Protein Low-Bind tubes. The beads can now be discarded as all peptides are in the supernatant.

steps.title: Halt tryptic activity by acidifying with...
steps.instruction: Halt tryptic activity by acidifying with 10 µL 5% v/v TFA.

steps.title: Prime C18 ZipTips by aspirating 100...
steps.instruction: Prime C18 ZipTips by aspirating 100 µL 50% ACN + 0.1% TFA. Discard solvent to waste. Repeat this step for a total of 2 primings.

steps.title: Wash the ZipTip by aspirating 100 µL 0
steps.instruction: Wash the ZipTip by aspirating 100 µL 0.1% TFA and discarding to waste. Repeat for a total of 2 washes.

steps.title: Desalt samples by aspirating and dispensing...
steps.instruction: Desalt samples by aspirating and dispensing 5-10 times in the sample. The peptides should now sit on the C18 filter of the ZipTip.

steps.title: Wash the ZipTip by aspirating 100 µL 0
steps.instruction: Wash the ZipTip by aspirating 100 µL 0.1% TFA and discarding to waste. Repeat for a total of 2 washes.

steps.title: Elute the peptides in a new...
steps.instruction: Elute the peptides in a new Low-Bind tube by taking up 100 µL 50% ACN + 0.1% TFA and dispensing. The peptides have now been eluted off the C18 filter into the solution.

steps.title: Preparing a 100 mM TCEP and 100 CAA solution
steps.instruction: Preparing a 100 mM TCEP and 100 CAA solution: Weigh out 28.66 mg TCEP and 9.35 mg CAA in a darkened tube (or one wrapped with foil). Add 1 mL ultrapure water and vortex for 1 minute to homogenise.

steps.title: Preparing a 50 µg/µL bead solution
steps.instruction: Preparing a 50 µg/µL bead solution

steps.title: Carefully invert the bottles of bead...
steps.instruction: Carefully invert the bottles of bead stock solutions and knock (!) them firmly on the counter to gain an equal distribution of the beads in the stock solution.

steps.title: Pipette 500 µL of the Sera-Mag...
steps.instruction: Pipette 500 µL of the Sera-Mag Speedbead carboxylate-modified [E3] magnetic particles Cytiva Life Sciences Catalog #65152105050250 into a tube and combine with 500 µL of Sera-Mag™ SpeedBead Carboxylate-Modified [E7] Magnetic Particles Cytiva Life Sciences Catalog #45152105050250 Place them on the magnetic rack and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash 3 times by removing the...
steps.instruction: Wash 3 times by removing the beads from the magnetic rack, resuspending in 1000 µL ultrapure water, homogenising through pipetting, placing back on the magnetic rack and removing the supernatant.

steps.title: After the final wash resuspend the...
steps.instruction: After the final wash resuspend the beads in 1000 µL ultrapure water and homogenise through gentle pipetting.

steps.title: Prepare foodcrust samples by sampling into new Protein Low-Bind tubes
steps.instruction: Prepare foodcrust samples by sampling into new Protein Low-Bind tubes. Suggested mass for each sample is 10-20 mg depending on sample availability.

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 13k RPM...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 13k RPM for 1 minute to pellet the foodcrust. Transfer the supernatant, which now contains all solubilised proteins, to a new tube. The remaining pellets can be discarded.
